title: 'Everything seemed to be cracking and melting': Former 'liquidator' recounts Chernobyl disaster, 40 years later
subhead: Halyna Kharshenko went to work at the Chernobyl nuclear power plant on April 26, 1986, unaware of the scale and severity of the explosion that had just occurred. The next day, she had to be rushed to a hospital unit where the staff would only approach her in full protective gear.

French hostages say they need more support after returning: 'Coming back was almost as hard as being detained. I felt isolated'
subhead: For many former French hostages and detainees held abroad, the ordeal continues upon their return to France, marked by a loss of bearings and a sense of abandonment.

title: How Tehran's propaganda lures the West into distraction
subhead: Pro-Iranian accounts are flooding the internet with AI-generated satirical videos that exploit Lego's popularity and Donald Trump's unpopularity, in an attempt to divert Western attention from the suffering of the Iranian people.

title: Five spots to bite into unforgettable madeleines
subhead: Check out our selection of bakeries and pastry shops where you can taste the finest examples of this highly Proustian and delightfully nostalgic cake.

title: 'Many see Trump's plans to reshape Washington as a metaphor for his governing style'
subhead: In an interview, Lawrence Vale, professor of urban planning at MIT, explains how the American president is striving to leave his mark on the federal capital through initiatives such as the Triumphal Arch, White House ballroom and statues.

'How can anyone love Céline Dion? The hysteria surrounding her Paris concerts is astonishing'
subhead: After the spectacular sale of nearly 500,000 tickets for a series of shows in Paris this autumn, Le Monde columnist Michel Guerrin questions in his column what drives people to love (or hate) the singer.

title: In Lebanon, Israel establishes an uninhabited buffer zone behind a 'yellow line'
subhead: The 'demarcation line' drawn by Israel on the third day of the ceasefire has revived memories of the 18-year occupation of Lebanon from 1982 to 2000. The Israeli army has banned Lebanese citizens from entering this 'defense zone,' which covers 6% of the country.

title: Weather sensors at Charles de Gaulle Airport at center of suspected manipulation on Polymarket
subhead: On the evenings of April 6 and April 15, temperatures recorded by a sensor at Paris Charles de Gaulle Airport spiked abnormally. Was it a weather phenomenon or an anomaly? There are strong suspicions of manipulation connected to online betting.
